candidate_score_status_reason: 2026-07-09 oracle fairness surgery removed 2 scored tests whose assertions measured uppercase-constructor-kwarg settings visibility, a mechanism the spec's closed Constructing Settings option list never promises (kept 51 -> 49). The shipped score_result.json is the untouched pre-surgery candidate run (36/51) and must be rerun against the 49-test oracle by a real candidate agent before use in analysis.

reference_gate.caveat: The 2 residual reference failures (test_cli_get_list_and_inspect_observe_configured_instance, test_cli_inspect_prints_json_report) are 20s CLI-subprocess timeouts: the scoring venv's `python -m dynaconf` cold start exceeded the oracle's internal timeout while this rerun shared the machine with concurrent reference runs. The pre-surgery 51/51 reference passed the same CLI tests, so these nodeids must not be treated as reference-unsolvable; a quiet-machine rerun is queued as an open item.

notes: 2026-07-09 fairness surgery: removed generated_tests.py::test_public_import_surface_exposes_configured_visible_state and generated_tests.py::test_cli_list_json_filters_by_key because their assertions targeted uppercase-constructor-kwarg settings projection, which the spec never promises. Pre-surgery bookkeeping snapshots live in archive/superseded-20260709/dynaconf-settings-fullrepro-001-oracle51/ (including the superseded 51/51 reference_score).

Four retained validator tests still use uppercase constructor kwargs as fixture plumbing; their assertions target promised validator semantics. The constructor-kwarg mechanism should be explicitly promised by the spec or the fixtures migrated to documented sources at the next respec cycle (open item, noted in spec_test_map.md).
